    8355              :         Buffer          buffer;
    8356              :         Page            page;
    8357              :         RelFileNode rnode;
    8358              :         BlockNumber blkno;
    8359              :         XLogRedoAction action;
    8360              : 
    8361            0 :         XLogRecGetBlockTag(record, 1, &rnode, NULL, &blkno);
    8362              : 
    8363              :         /*
    8364              :          * If there are any Hot Standby transactions running that have an xmin
    8365              :          * horizon old enough that this page isn't all-visible for them, they
    8366              :          * might incorrectly decide that an index-only scan can skip a heap fetch.
    8367              :          *
    8368              :          * NB: It might be better to throw some kind of "soft" conflict here that
    8369              :          * forces any index-only scan that is in flight to perform heap fetches,
    8370              :          * rather than killing the transaction outright.
    8371              :          */
    8372            0 :         if (InHotStandby)
    8373            0 :                 ResolveRecoveryConflictWithSnapshot(xlrec->cutoff_xid, rnode);
    8374              : 
    8375              :         /*
    8376              :          * Read the heap page, if it still exists. If the heap file has dropped or
    8377              :          * truncated later in recovery, we don't need to update the page, but we'd
    8378              :          * better still update the visibility map.
    8379              :          */
    8380            0 :         action = XLogReadBufferForRedo(record, 1, &buffer);
    8381            0 :         if (action == BLK_NEEDS_REDO)
    8382              :         {
    8383              :                 /*
    8384              :                  * We don't bump the LSN of the heap page when setting the visibility
    8385              :                  * map bit (unless checksums or wal_hint_bits is enabled, in which
    8386              :                  * case we must). This exposes us to torn page hazards, but since
    8387              :                  * we're not inspecting the existing page contents in any way, we
    8388              :                  * don't care.
    8389              :                  *
    8390              :                  * However, all operations that clear the visibility map bit *do* bump
    8391              :                  * the LSN, and those operations will only be replayed if the XLOG LSN
    8392              :                  * follows the page LSN.  Thus, if the page LSN has advanced past our
    8393              :                  * XLOG record's LSN, we mustn't mark the page all-visible, because
    8394              :                  * the subsequent update won't be replayed to clear the flag.

    8396            0 :                 page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
    8397              : 
    8398            0 :                 PageSetAllVisible(page);
    8399              : 
    8400            0 :                 if (XLogHintBitIsNeeded())
    8401            0 :                         PageSetLSN(page, lsn);
    8402              : 
    8403            0 :                 MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
    8404              :         }
    8405              :         else if (action == BLK_RESTORED)
    8406              :         {
    8407              :                 /*
    8408              :                  * If heap block was backed up, we already restored it and there's
    8409              :                  * nothing more to do. (This can only happen with checksums or
    8410              :                  * wal_log_hints enabled.)
    8411              :                  */
    8412              :         }
    8413              : 
    8414            0 :         if (BufferIsValid(buffer))
    8415              :         {
    8416            0 :                 Size            space = PageGetFreeSpace(BufferGetPage(buffer));
    8417              : 
    8418            0 :                 UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
    8419              : 
    8420              :                 /*
    8421              :                  * Since FSM is not WAL-logged and only updated heuristically, it
    8422              :                  * easily becomes stale in standbys.  If the standby is later promoted
    8423              :                  * and runs VACUUM, it will skip updating individual free space
    8424              :                  * figures for pages that became all-visible (or all-frozen, depending
    8425              :                  * on the vacuum mode,) which is troublesome when FreeSpaceMapVacuum
    8426              :                  * propagates too optimistic free space values to upper FSM layers;
    8427              :                  * later inserters try to use such pages only to find out that they
    8428              :                  * are unusable.  This can cause long stalls when there are many such
    8429              :                  * pages.
    8430              :                  *
    8431              :                  * Forestall those problems by updating FSM's idea about a page that
    8432              :                  * is becoming all-visible or all-frozen.
    8433              :                  *
    8434              :                  * Do this regardless of a full-page image being applied, since the
    8435              :                  * FSM data is not in the page anyway.
    8436              :                  */
    8437            0 :                 if (xlrec->flags & VISIBILITYMAP_VALID_BITS)
    8438            0 :                         XLogRecordPageWithFreeSpace(rnode, blkno, space);
    8439              :         }
    8440              : 
    8441              :         /*
    8442              :          * Even if we skipped the heap page update due to the LSN interlock, it's
    8443              :          * still safe to update the visibility map.  Any WAL record that clears
    8444              :          * the visibility map bit does so before checking the page LSN, so any
    8445              :          * bits that need to be cleared will still be cleared.
    8446              :          */
    8447            0 :         if (XLogReadBufferForRedoExtended(record, 0, RBM_ZERO_ON_ERROR, false,
    8448              :                                                                           &vmbuffer) == BLK_NEEDS_REDO)
    8449              :         {
    8450            0 :                 Page            vmpage = BufferGetPage(vmbuffer);
    8451              :                 Relation        reln;
    8452              : 
    8453              :                 /* initialize the page if it was read as zeros */
    8454            0 :                 if (PageIsNew(vmpage))
    8455            0 :                         PageInit(vmpage, BLCKSZ, 0);
    8456              : 
    8457              :                 /*
    8458              :                  * XLogReadBufferForRedoExtended locked the buffer. But

    8461            0 :                 LockBuffer(vmbuffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
    8462              : 
    8463            0 :                 reln = CreateFakeRelcacheEntry(rnode);
    8464            0 :                 visibilitymap_pin(reln, blkno, &vmbuffer);
    8465              : 
    8466              :                 /*

    8479            0 :                                                           xlrec->cutoff_xid, xlrec->flags);
    8480              : 
    8481            0 :                 ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
    8482            0 :                 FreeFakeRelcacheEntry(reln);
    8483              :         }
    8484            0 :         else if (BufferIsValid(vmbuffer))
    8485            0 :                 UnlockReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);

    9548              : /*
    9549              :  * HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut
    9550              :  *              We are reading a tuple.  If it's not visible, there may be a
    9551              :  *              rw-conflict out with the inserter.  Otherwise, if it is visible to us
    9552              :  *              but has been deleted, there may be a rw-conflict out with the deleter.
    9553              :  *
    9554              :  * We will determine the top level xid of the writing transaction with which
    9555              :  * we may be in conflict, and ask CheckForSerializableConflictOut() to check
    9556              :  * for overlap with our own transaction.
    9557              :  *
    9558              :  * This function should be called just about anywhere in heapam.c where a
    9559              :  * tuple has been read. The caller must hold at least a shared lock on the
    9560              :  * buffer, because this function might set hint bits on the tuple. There is
    9561              :  * currently no known reason to call this function from an index AM.
    9562              :  */

    9565              :                                                                         HeapTuple tuple, Buffer buffer,

    9568              :         TransactionId xid;

    9572       533897 :                 return;
    9573              : 
    9574              :         /*
    9575              :          * Check to see whether the tuple has been written to by a concurrent
    9576              :          * transaction, either to create it not visible to us, or to delete it

    9578              :          * tuple is visible to us, while HeapTupleSatisfiesVacuum checks what else
    9579              :          * is going on with it.

    9593            0 :                 case HEAPTUPLE_RECENTLY_DEAD:
